text:
Market today: Mood is cautious but not panicked. The real driver for your names is Apple flagging a memory crunch and hinting at more price hikes — that's a direct chip/semiconductor signal that cuts both ways: pricing power for suppliers but demand risk if end-markets slow. Watch how semis react.

Your holdings: PLTR is down 14% and flagged SELL — on margin, that hole gets deeper fast, no headline catalyst today to save it. AVGO is your largest position, down nearly 11% and flagged TRIM — the Apple memory note is adjacent, but this needs sizing down regardless. SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF already down 11.5% with daily decay eating you alive; SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record — both SpaceX plays need tight manual management. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: (1) Semiconductor tape after the Apple memory headline — does it lift AVGO or spook it? (2) Your PLTR stop at $85 — know your exit before the open.

Bottom line: Your two biggest problems are PLTR and AVGO — act on the scanner signals before margin turns a bad week into a bad month.
